title: Use of Cryptography — Weak Cipher Suites Negotiated on Public Load Balancer

spl:
index=lb sourcetype=f5:ltm earliest=-7d
| where clientssl_cipher IN ("RC4-SHA","DES-CBC3-SHA") OR match(clientssl_cipher,"TLS_RSA")
| stats count by virtual_name, clientssl_cipher
description: Parses TLS handshake logs for deprecated ciphers on internet-facing VIPs supporting cryptography policy.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes lb and sourcetypes f5:ltm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Use of Cryptography — Weak Cipher Suites Negotiated on Public Load Balancer. ISO 27001 tells us what good security management looks like, and we document that we follow it.
implementation: (1) Align with crypto standard; (2) phased deprecation plan; (3) external scan cross-check. Indexes required: `index=lb`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=f5:ltm`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: F5 LTM logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `f5:ltm`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align with crypto standard; (2) phased deprecation plan; (3) external scan cross-check.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=lb sourcetype=f5:ltm earliest=-7d
| where clientssl_cipher IN ("RC4-SHA","DES-CBC3-SHA") OR match(clientssl_cipher,"TLS_RSA")
| stats count by virtual_name, clientssl_cipher
```

Understanding this SPL

**Use of Cryptography — Weak Cipher Suites Negotiated on Public Load Balancer (A.8.24)** — Parses TLS handshake logs for deprecated ciphers on internet-facing VIPs supporting cryptography policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `f5:ltm`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): F5 LTM logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: lb; **sourcetype**: f5:ltm. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=lb, sourcetype=f5:ltm, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where clientssl_cipher IN ("RC4-SHA","DES-CBC3-SHA") OR match(clientssl_cipher,"TLS_RSA")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by virtual_name, clientssl_cipher** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart, Table
knownFalsePositives: Incidents may bunch during major events, vulnerability bulletin response, or controlled exercises.
